metadata.title: Acme Health Patient Intake API - CMMC Component Definition

components.title: Patient Intake API
components.description: Serverless ingestion engine for PHI, utilizing AWS Lambda, DynamoDB, and S3, hardened against CMMC Level 2 controls.
components.purpose: Securely ingest patient data and provide immutable audit trails.

control-implementations.description: Technical control implementation managed via Terraform and verified by OPA/Rego policies.

implemented-requirements.description: FIPS-validated cryptography (KMS) is enforced for all CUI/PHI storage.

statements.description: Encryption is managed via Customer Managed Keys (CMK) with rotation enabled. Default AWS managed keys are prohibited.

implemented-requirements.description: Boundary protection via VPC isolation and Interface Endpoints.

statements.description: The intake Lambda is isolated within private subnets. All service communication (S3, DynamoDB, KMS) occurs over PrivateLink VPC Endpoints with no public internet egress.

props.name: egress-protection
props.value: Security Group limited to VPC CIDR only

implemented-requirements.description: Data in transit is protected via mandatory TLS 1.2+ enforcement.

statements.description: S3 bucket policies explicitly deny non-HTTPS (aws:SecureTransport = false) requests.

implemented-requirements.description: Least privilege is enforced on all service-to-service identities.

statements.description: IAM inline policies use resource-level ARNs and specific actions, eliminating wildcards for sensitive operations.

implemented-requirements.description: Protection of media (PHI) via versioning and Object Lock.

statements.description: Versioning ensures data recovery from accidental overwrites. The evidence vault utilizes GOVERNANCE mode Object Lock.
